questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A gas mixture consists of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> moles of oxygen and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> moles of argon at temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math>. Assuming the gases to be ideal and the oxygen bond to be rigid, the total internal energy (in units of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mi>T</mi></math>) of the mixutre is :
questions.solution:
f1n1RT12+f2n2RT22

=3×52RT+52×3RT=15
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The plot that depicts the behavior of the mean free time <math><mi>τ</mi></math> (time between two successive collisions) for the molecules of an ideal gas, as a function of temperature <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>T</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> qualitatively, is: (Graphs are schematic and not drawn to scale)
questions.solution:
The average time taken by a moving particle between successive collisions or impacts is referred to as the mean free time τ.

The average distance a moving particle travels between successive collisions or impacts is referred to as the mean free path λ.

λ=kT2πd2P

and vrms=3RTM

So, the mean free time τ=λvrms

Therefore, τ∝1T
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two ideal polyatomic gases at temperatures <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>T</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>T</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are mixed so that there is no loss of energy. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>F</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi></mrow><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>n</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>n</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be the degrees of freedom, masses, number of molecules of the first and second gas respectively, the temperature of mixture of these two gases is:
questions.solution:
Let the final temperature of the mixture be T.

Since, there is no loss in energy.

ΔU=0

⇒F12n1RΔT+F22n2RΔT=0

⇒F12n1RT1-T+F22n2RT2-T=0

⇒T=F1n1RT1+F2n2RT2 F1n1R+F2n2R=F1n1 T1+F2n2 T2 F1n1+F2n2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Consider a gas of triatomic molecules. The molecules  are assumed to be triangular and made of massless rigid rods whose vertices are occupied by atoms. The internal energy of a mole of the gas at temperature T is:
questions.solution:
The formula of internal energy of a gas is given by

U=f2RT

Here R is the gas constant and T is temperature.

R=8.314 J mol-1 K-1

For the given configuration of triatomic molecules, the degree of freedom f=6

⇒ U=62RT=3RT
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a dilute gas at pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> and temperature '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></math>', the time between successive collision of a molecule varies with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> as :
questions.solution:
Tmean α1T
∴ time =V4π2r2VN
V=2RTπM
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If one mole of the polyatomic gas is having two vibrational modes and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> is the ratio of molar specific heats for polyatomic gas <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi></msub></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> is :
questions.solution:
f=4+3+3=10, assuming non-linear, 

β=CpCv=1+2f=1210=1.2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Consider a mixture of gas molecule of types <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> having masses <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></msub><mo>.</mo></math> The ratio of their root mean square speeds at normal temperature and pressure is:
questions.solution:
Vrms=3RTM
mA<mB<mC
⇒VA>VB>VC
⇒1 VA<1 VB<1 VC
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A gas has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> degrees of freedom. The ratio of specific heat of gas at constant volume to the specific heat of gas at constant pressure will be
questions.solution:
For a gas having degrees of freedom n,

CV=nR2 and using Mayer's relation CP=CV+R. Therefore,

CVCP=nn+2.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Same gas is filled in two vessels of the same volume at the same temperature. If the ratio of the number of molecules is 1:4, then

A. The r.m.s. velocity of gas molecules in two vessels will be the same.

B. The ratio of pressure in these vessels will be 1:4.

C. The ratio of pressure will be 1:1.

D. The r.m.s. velocity of gas molecules in two vessels will be in the ratio of 1:4.
questions.solution:
The rms velocity of a gas is given by Vrms=3RTM. Therefore,Vrms is independent of number of moles, Hence, statement A is correct and D is incorrect.

According to the ideal gas equation PV=nRT⇒P∝n

⇒P1P2=n1 n2=14

Hence, statement B is correct and C is incorrect.

Ans [A & B only are correct]
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Which statements are correct about degrees of freedom?

A. A molecule with n degrees of freedom has n2 different ways of storing energy.

B. Each degree of freedom is associated with 12RT average energy per mole.

C. A monoatomic gas molecule has 1 rotational degree of freedom where as diatomic molecule has 2 rotational degrees of freedom

D. CH4 has a total to 6 degrees of freedom.

Choose the correct answer from the option given below:
questions.solution:
According to the equipartition of energy, each degree of freedom carries 12RT energy per mole and total energy per mole of a gas having n degrees of freedom is 12nRT. Therefore, statement A is wrong and statement B is correct.

A monoatomic molecule only has translational degrees of freedom and does not have rotational degree of freedom. For a polyatomic model there are three rotational degrees of freedom and three translational degrees of freedom. Therefore, statement C is incorrect and statement D is correct.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A mixture of hydrogen and oxygen has volume 2000 cm3, temperature 300 K, pressure 100kPa and mass 0.76 g. The ratio of number of moles of hydrogen to number of moles of oxygen in the mixture will be

[Take gas constant R=8.3 J K-1 mol-1]
questions.solution:
From the ideal gas equation, total number of moles in the mixture are,

n=PVRT=2×10-3×100×103×325×300⇒n=225=0.08

If number of moles of oxygen are n1 and of hydrogen are n2

n1+n2=n=0.08…1

And total mass of the mixture

n132+n22=0.76…2

Solving the above equations,

n1=0.02 and n2=0.06

n2n1=3
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The number of air molecules per cm3 is increased from 3×1019 to 12×1019. The ratio of collision frequency of air molecules before and after the increase in number respectively is :
questions.solution:
The given data is 

n1=3×1019n2=12×1019

The mean time between two collision has the meaning of a period τ, since it indicates the repetitive time intervals in which on average collisions take place. The reciprocal of the time τ can be understood as the collision frequency f, which indicates the number of collisions per unit time.The collision frequency is also directly proportional to the number molecules per unit volume.

f∝1τ

⇒f∝n

⇒f1 f2 =n1n2=3×101912×1019=0.25
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The mean free path of molecules of a certain gas at STP is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1500</mn><mi>d</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi><mo> </mo></math>is the diameter of the gas molecules. While maintaining the standard pressure, the mean free path of the molecules at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>373</mn><mo> </mo><mi>K</mi></math> is approximately:
questions.solution:
The mean free path is given by the relation,

λ=RT2πd2NAP

So, λ∝T

Let λ' be the new mean free path.

Hence,

1500dλ'=273373

⇒λ'=2049.45d≈2049d
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The rms speed of oxygen molecule in a vessel at particular temperature is 1+5x12v, when v is the average speed of the molecule. The value of x will be:

(take π=227)
questions.solution:
The RMS speed is given by 

vrms=3RTM

The average speed is given by 

vavg=8RTπM

The ratio is 

⇒vrmsvavg =3RTM8RTπM=3π8=38×227⇒vrmsvavg =3328

Comparing it with the given value 1+5x12,

vrmsvavg =1+528⇒x=28
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A calorimeter of water equivalent <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> contains <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><none></none><mo>'</mo><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mo>'</mo></mmultiscripts></math>' grams of steam at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is mixed in it till the temperature of the mixture is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>31</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><none></none><mo>'</mo><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mo>'</mo></mmultiscripts></math> is close to (Latent heat of water<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mn>540</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cal</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, specific heat of water<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cal</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
20031-25=m×540+m169

1200=m609

m≈2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two thin metallic spherical shells of radii <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> are placed with their centres coinciding. A material of thermal conductivity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> is filled in the space between the shells. The inner shell is maintained at temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and the outer shell at temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>θ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> The rate at which heat flows radially through the material is :
questions.solution:
Equivalent resistance (R)=r2-r1 K4πr2r1
Thus, Heat flow =4πKr1r2θ2-θ1r2-r1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two metallic blocks M1 and M2 of same area of cross-section are connected to each other (as shown in figure). If the thermal conductivity of M2 is K then the thermal conductivity of M1 will be : [Assume steady state heat conduction]
questions.solution:
Let thermal conductivity of M1 be K'.

Using heat current relation for steady state conduction,

K'A100-8016=KA80-08

⇒K'×2016=K×808⇒K'=8K
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A cylinder of fixed capacity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>44</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn></math> litres contains helium gas at standard temperature and pressure. The amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of gas in the cylinder by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> will be (Given gas constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi>mol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
At STP volume occupied by one mole of a gas is 22.4 L. Therefore, number of moles are,  n=44.822.4=2 mol.

The process is isochoric process as the volume of the cylinder remains fixed and helium is a monatomic gas. Therefore, amount of heat needed will be,

Q=nCvΔT=232R20=3×8.3×20=498 J
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
At what temperature a gold ring of diameter 6.230 cm be heated so that it can be fitted on a wooden bangle of diameter 6.241 cm? Both the diameters have been measured at room temperature 27°C.
(Given: coefficient of linear thermal expansion of gold αL=1.4×10-5 K-1)
questions.solution:
To fit gold ring of diameter 5.230 cm over wooden bangle of diameter 6.241 cm, gold ring expansion is required.

So, l=l01+αΔT

⇒6.241=6.2301+1.4×10-5T-27

⇒6.2416.230-1=1.4×10-5T-27

⇒T=152.7°C
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The graph between two temperature scales P and Q is shown in the figure. Between upper fixed point and lower fixed point there are 150 equal divisions of scale P and 100 divisions on scale Q. The relationship for conversion between the two scales is given by :

.
questions.solution:
From the graph

For scale P,
Lower fixed point=180-150=30°P

Upper fixed point=180°P

For scale Q,
Lower fixed point=0°Q

Upper fixed point=100°Q

Thus, the relationship between the two scales P and Q is given by

tP-30180-30=tQ-0100-0

⇒tP-30150=tQ100
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> of water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is converted into steam at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> by boiling at atmospheric pressure. The volume of water changes from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>00</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> as a liquid to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>671</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> as steam. The change in internal energy of the system during the process will be (Given latent heat of vaporisation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>2257</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kJ</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>kg</mi></math>, Atmospheric pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open=""><mrow><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>5</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Pa</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
The work to be done in the process is given by

dW=PdV=1×105 Pa×(1.671-0.001) m3=1.670×105 J

The change in heat energy during the vaporisation process can be calculated as follows-

ΔQsupplied =2257×1×103 J=22.57×105 J

Hence, the change in internal energy in the process is given by

ΔU=ΔQsupplied-ΔW=(22.57-1.67)×105 J=20.9×105 J=2090 kJ
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Three different processes that can occur in an ideal monoatomic gas are shown in the P vs V diagram. The paths are labelled as A→B,A→C and A→D. The change in internal energies during these process are taken as EAB,EAC and EAD and the work done as WAB,WAC and WAD. The correct relation between these parameters are:
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><mn>0</mn></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">B</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></mrow></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>W</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="italic">A</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">D</mi></mrow></msub></math>
questions.solution:
EAB=EAC=EAD

dU=nfR2 Tf-Ti

WAB>0(+) as V↑

WAC=0 as V=constant

WAD<0(-) as V↓

ΔT is same for EAB=EAC=EAD
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A thermodynamic cycle xyzx is shown on a V - T diagram.

The P - V diagram that best describes this cycle is: (Diagrams are schematic and not to scale)
questions.solution:
According to the ideal equation,

PV=nRT

In the given V‐T diagram,

In process x→y, V∝T. So, the process is isobaric process.

In process y→z, V= constant. So, the process is an isochoric process.

In process z→x, T= constant. So, the process is an isothermal process.

Analyzing using the ideal gas equation in each process, the P‐V graph can be drawn as shown below,
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Which one is the correct option for the two different thermodynamic processes ?
questions.solution:
Option (a) is wrong; since in adiabatic process V≠ constant.

Option (b) is wrong, since in the isothermal process
T= constant

Option (c) & (d) matches isotherms & adiabatic formula :

TVγ-1= constant &Tγpγ-1=constant
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Which of the following graphs represent the behaviour of an ideal gas? Symbols have their usual meaning.

PV∝T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: If one mole of an ideal gas at P1,V1 is allowed to expand reversibly and isothermally (A to B) its pressure is reduced to one-half of the original pressure (see figure). This is followed by a constant volume cooling till its pressure is reduced to one-fourth of the initial value B→C. Then it is restored to its initial state by a reversible adiabatic compression (C to A). The net workdone by the gas is equal to:
questions.solution:
A-B= isothermal process

WAB=P1V1 ln2V1 V1=P1V1ln2

B-C→ Isochoric process

WBC=0

C-A→Adiabatic process

WCA=P1V1-P14×2V11-γ=P1V11-121-γ=P1V121-γ

Wnet=WAB+WBC+WCA  P1V1=RT

=P1V1 ln2+0+P1V121-γ

Wnet=RT ln2-12γ-1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: One mole of an ideal gas is taken through an adiabatic process where the temperature rises from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>37</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. If the ideal gas is composed of polyatomic molecule that has 4 vibrational modes, which of the following is true? <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>314</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>
options: work done by the gas is close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>332</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>
work done on the gas is close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>582</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>
work done by the gas is close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>582</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>
work done on the gas is close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>332</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Since, each vibrational mode, corresponds to two degrees of freedom, hence, f=3 (trans.) +3( rot. )+8( vib. )=14

&  γ=1+2f

γ=1+214=87

W=nRΔTγ-1=-582

As W<0. work is done on the gas.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A certain amount of gas of volume <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> temperature and pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>7</mn></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> expands isothermally until its volume gets doubled. Later it expands adiabatically until its volume gets redoubled. The final pressure of the gas will be (Use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math>)
questions.solution:
Given here, P1=2×107 N m-2=2×107 Pa

Using Boyle's law,

P1V1=P2V2

Since V2=2V1.

Hence, P2=P12 (isothermal expansion)

P2=1×107 Pa

Now, during adiabatic expansion, 

P2V2γ=P32V2γ

P3=1×10721.5=3.536×106 Pa
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In an Isothermal change, the change in pressure and volume of a gas can be represented for three different temperature; <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>T</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi>T</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>></mo><msub><mi>T</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> as:
questions.solution:
In case of isothermal process, pressure vs volume graph is a rectangular hyperbola.

The graph of the isothermal process shifts away from the origin as the temperature of the process is increased. Thus, the curve away from the origin T3 have the highest temperature whereas the curve closer to the origin T1 have the lowest temperature.

Here, the dotted line shows the isobaric line that implies T3>T2>T1 as T=PVnR.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>γ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> be the ratio of molar specific heat at constant pressure and molar specific heat at constant volume of a monoatomic gas and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>γ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be the similar ratio of diatomic gas. Considering the diatomic gas molecule as a rigid rotator, the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>γ</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>γ</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is:
questions.solution:
As we know, Cv=fR2

and Cp=Cv+R=fR2+R

Therefore, γ=CpCv=1+2f

For monatomic gas, f=3 and hence γ1=53

For diatomic gas, f=5 and hence γ2=75

Required ratio, γ1γ2=2521
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: A hypothetical gas expands adiabatically such that its volume changes from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>08</mn></math> litres to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn></math> litres. If the ratio of final pressure of the gas to initial pressure of the gas is<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>16</mn><mn>81</mn></mfrac></math> . Then the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>p</mi></msub><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>v</mi></msub></mfrac></math> will be.
questions.solution:
Let γ be the ratio of CpCv.

Then for adiabatic process PVγ =Constant

⇒PiPf=VfViγ

⇒8116=278γ⇒32343=278γ

⇒γ=43
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
